warnings: This record is a MET observation of AI programming work items routed through AEX and PQX. It does not claim AEX admitted, PQX executed, EVL evaluated, CDE decided, or SEL enforced.
AEX is the canonical admission authority for repo-mutating execution. PQX is the canonical execution authority. EVL owns eval. CDE owns control. SEL owns enforcement.
Source signals are seeded from preflight PQX slice execution evidence and the dashboard_seed loop. Coverage is partial; unknowns remain visible by design.
failure_prevented: AI programming changes (Codex, Claude) entering the repo without observable AEX admission or PQX execution evidence being read as governed when they are not.
signal_improved: Dashboard exposes whether each AI programming work item carries AEX admission evidence and PQX execution evidence, with bypass risk surfaced when proof is absent.

ai_programming_work_items.next_recommended_input: Attach AEX admission evidence (admitted_slices reference, admission decision, source_artifacts_used) for Codex work item AIPG-CODEX-001 so the governed path through AEX can be observed end-to-end.

Attach PQX execution evidence (pqx_slice_execution_record reference, execution_status, source_artifacts_used) for Claude work item AIPG-CLAUDE-001 so the governed path through PQX can be observed end-to-end.
Identify the AI programming agent type (codex | claude | other) for work item AIPG-UNKNOWN-001 and attach AEX admission plus PQX execution evidence so the governed path can be observed.

next_recommended_input: Aggregate roll-up across ai_programming_work_items: AEX is present in all items, but PQX, TPA, and SEL are missing in at least one repo-mutating item. Emit PQX execution records, TPA trust/policy signals, and SEL enforcement signals before any work item can be promoted.
